content: If we treat LLM self-reflection as a high school English writing workshop revision process, the original Reflexion agent’s single-pass self-critique can be reframed as a peer editing exercise where the LLM acts as both the writer and the critical reviewer, with graded feedback from the ‘teacher’ (task environment) refining its self-reflection habits over time. This framing suggests that multi-turn iterative self-reflection in LLMs benefits from the same structured rhetorical feedback loops that improve student writing, rather than just informal internal logical checks.
